sprint.title: Account Security v1: Set Password Flow + Google Sign-In UX (Account Chooser) + Branded Button
sprint.goal: Add a Set Password flow for OAuth/OTP users who don't have a password, and improve Google login UX: show an account-chooser step and ensure the 'Continue with Google' button follows Google branding (official text/icon/theme).
definitionOfDone: If a user logs in via Google/OTP and has no password, they are routed to /account/set-password based on policy
Set Password page sets a password securely and updates user profile hasPassword=true
React Email template exists and is sent for 'Password set successfully'
Google login UI uses compliant 'Continue with Google' button styling and icon
Google login supports account chooser behavior (user can pick which Google account) and does not silently reuse an existing session
No redirect loops; returnTo works; skip policy works if enabled
Automated tests cover routing, setPassword, and Google button rendering

stackAssumptions.oauth: Google Identity Services / OAuth configuration via Convex Auth provider
stackAssumptions.design: You want a separate page for Google account selection UX before starting OAuth

googleBranding: Google recommends using the Google-rendered 'Sign in with Google' button via Google Identity Services to ensure branding compliance.
If rendering yourself, you must use approved assets and allowed button text like 'Continue with Google'.

backlog.title: Define Set Password policy and post-login routing rules

requirements.v1Decision: Optional prompt after OAuth/OTP login + required gate before Export (config-driven).
routingRules: After successful auth, if hasPassword=false and policy requires prompt -> route to /account/set-password
Preserve returnTo to send user back to intended destination after set/skip
constraints: Do not block story answering for elders

acceptanceTests: Policy is defined in one config location
Routing behavior is deterministic and loop-free

backlog.title: Detect password presence (hasPassword) and persist it in user profile

behavior: Determine if a user has a password credential linked (via Convex Auth identity/provider info you maintain)
Update flag when password is set/changed
constraints: Do not store password hashes yourself
Works for Google OAuth + OTP users

backlog.title: Set Password UI: elder-friendly form (New/Confirm) + show/hide + success

ui: Secondary: Skip for now (if policy allows)

acceptanceTests: User can set password and is redirected back
Skip works and returns user back

backlog.title: Backend action: set password for current user + rate limit + audit event

behavior: Set password credential via Convex Auth password provider APIs
security: If user already has password: return error (or redirect to Change Password in future)

acceptanceTests: OAuth/OTP user can set password once
Rate limit triggers on repeated attempts

backlog.title: React Email template: Password Set Success (branded) + send on success

send: After setPassword success, send email via sender layer

acceptanceTests: Email send is triggered after password set

backlog.title: Google Sign-In button branding compliance (Continue with Google + icon + theme)

rules: Prefer Google-rendered button via Google Identity Services where possible (branding always up-to-date).
If using custom button, use approved text options including 'Continue with Google' and the official Google 'G' mark per guidelines.
ui: Primary Google auth button uses 'Continue with Google' label and Google icon
Button sizing consistent with other auth CTAs
Support dark/light themes using allowed button themes
constraints: Do not alter the Google logo

acceptanceTests: Google button matches branding requirements and uses allowed text 'continue_with'
Button displays correctly in light/dark mode

backlog.title: Google account chooser step (intermediate page) before OAuth redirect

requirements.goal: When user clicks Google login, show a short intermediate page that helps them choose which Google account will be used (especially useful on shared devices).
ui: Short copy: 'Choose the Google account you want to use.'
oauthBehavior: Force Google account chooser by using appropriate OAuth parameters (e.g., prompt=select_account) where supported by your OAuth integration stack.
constraints: If provider forces a picker anyway, this page still adds clarity and reduces surprise
Avoid collecting Google account list yourself (do not attempt to enumerate accounts)

acceptanceTests: Clicking Google on sign-in routes to choose-google-account page
Continuing triggers OAuth and shows Google account selection when multiple accounts exist

backlog.title: Sensitive action gate (optional): require password before Export if configured

gates: If hasPassword=false and policy says requiredForExport=true, show modal prompting Set Password

acceptanceTests: Export prompts Set Password when required

backlog.title: QA + automated tests (set password routing, google chooser, google button)

manualChecklist: Google login → chooser page → OAuth → logged in
User without password → routed to set-password (or prompted) → set password → redirected back
Google button styling correct and consistent
Dark mode button theme is acceptable
automation: Unit: postLoginRedirect chooses set-password when hasPassword=false
Integration: google chooser page sets OAuth params (prompt=select_account) in redirect URL

acceptanceTests: At least 5 automated tests added and passing

notes: Google recommends using the Google-rendered button via Google Identity Services to ensure branding compliance and support allowed text like 'continue_with'.
Account selection is typically controlled by OAuth parameters (prompt=select_account) depending on the implementation.
